key_insight: The paper introduces the Patnaik-Pearson intrinsic dimension as a new way to quantify the geometry of neural-network representations, and shows it aligns with power-law spectral behavior in some regimes.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a geometry-based diagnostic for tracking how representation complexity changes across transformer layers and comparing models.
method: Defines a new intrinsic-dimension estimator inspired by TwoNN and related HTSR/SETOL ideas.
Proves theoretical properties of the estimator and analyzes how it behaves under common neural-network transformations.
Applies it to weight matrices and token embeddings in BERT-base and DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-1.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports both theoretical analysis and numerical experiments on transformer models, including embedding manifolds at input and across layers, with code released publicly.
watch_item: Results may depend on assumptions such as power-law empirical spectral densities and on estimator sensitivity to representation scaling or preprocessing.
paper_title: Patnaik-Pearson intrinsic dimension for internal representations of neural networks
